mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter signals easier financial conditions, lower discount rates, and higher equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more than 55% of the time when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and impending earnings downgrades, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect becomes positive in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, supporting equity earnings expectations and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if rising RSAFS fails to coincide with positive 60-day forward SPX returns in at least 55% of observed instances, or if the rolling 2-year effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in INDPRO relative to its recent monthly level signals slowing industrial output and weaker cyclically sensitive earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Rejected if below-trend INDPRO episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency across a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is weak while RSAFS remains strong, the market reads the data mix as a housing-specific slowdown without broad demand collapse, which should support mildly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition of weak HOUST and stable-to-strong RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS growth indicates a still-tight labor market with resilient payroll expansion, which should support earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint labor-strength condition fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term becomes insignificant in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, an elevated FEDFUNDS rate above neutral raises discount rates and financing costs, and this effect becomes stronger when CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if high-rate, high-inflation episodes do not deliver negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the joint effect is not stronger than FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle slowdown, falling GDPC1 together with weak PAYEMS indicates a real activity deceleration that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings compression and risk-off repricing.
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the sign reverses during late-cycle periods.
